questions.question: What is an election?
questions.correct_answer: A process through which people choose their representatives in a democracy
questions.explanation: Elections allow citizens to select leaders who will represent them and run the government.

questions.question: Why are elections necessary in a democracy?
questions.correct_answer: They allow people to choose and change their representatives
questions.explanation: Elections ensure that leaders remain accountable to the people and can be replaced if necessary.

questions.question: What is political competition?
questions.correct_answer: Competition among political parties and candidates to win elections
questions.explanation: Political competition motivates leaders to work for the welfare of the people in order to gain votes.

questions.question: What is an electoral constituency?
questions.correct_answer: A geographical area represented by one elected representative
questions.explanation: Voters living in a constituency elect one representative to the legislative body.

questions.question: What is a voters list?
questions.correct_answer: An official list of all eligible voters in a constituency
questions.explanation: Only people whose names appear on the voters list can vote in an election.

questions.question: Who can vote in elections in India?
questions.correct_answer: All citizens aged 18 years and above
questions.explanation: This principle is called universal adult franchise.

questions.question: What is an election campaign?
questions.correct_answer: Activities by candidates and parties to persuade voters to support them
questions.explanation: Campaigns include rallies, speeches, advertisements, and discussions on public issues.

questions.question: What is the role of the Election Commission of India?
questions.correct_answer: To conduct free and fair elections in the country
questions.explanation: The Election Commission supervises elections, enforces rules, and ensures fairness.

questions.question: What are reserved constituencies?
questions.correct_answer: Constituencies reserved for candidates from Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes
questions.explanation: Reservation ensures representation of weaker sections in legislative bodies.

questions.question: What is meant by free and fair elections?
questions.correct_answer: Elections where voters can choose their representatives without pressure or fraud
questions.explanation: Free and fair elections ensure equality, transparency, and democratic legitimacy.

notes.title: Importance of Elections
notes.content: Elections allow citizens to choose their leaders and hold them accountable. They are a key feature of democratic governance.

notes.title: Conditions of Democratic Elections
notes.content: Democratic elections require universal adult franchise, political competition, regular elections, and free and fair voting.

notes.title: Electoral Constituencies
notes.content: The country is divided into constituencies so that each representative is responsible for a specific geographical area.

notes.title: Election Campaign
notes.content: Election campaigns allow candidates to communicate their policies and convince voters to support them.

notes.title: Election Commission
notes.content: The Election Commission of India is an independent body responsible for conducting elections and ensuring they are fair and transparent.

facts.fact: India has one of the largest democratic electorates in the world.
Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) are used in India to record votes.
Every vote has equal value in a democratic election.
The Election Commission can order repolling if unfair practices are found.
